report_name: Coinbase Cartel Ransomware Group Claims Attack on Engineering Firm Aptim, Threatens Data Leak

threat_actors.name: Coinbase Cartel
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against Aptim, using double extortion tactics and demonstrating technical knowledge by leaking a Kerberos hash.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment and data leakage threats.

attack_patterns.name: Kerberoasting
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor likely used Kerberoasting to compromise service account credentials within Aptim's Active Directory environment, as evidenced by a leaked Kerberos pre-authentication hash.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: After cracking the service account credential obtained via Kerberoasting, the attackers would have used the valid account for lateral movement within the network.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The attackers collected and exfiltrated sensitive data from Aptim's network to their own servers before deploying ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Coinbase Cartel group deployed their ransomware to encrypt systems on Aptim's network as part of their double extortion strategy.

malware.name: Coinbase Cartel Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware deployed by the Coinbase Cartel group to encrypt systems at Aptim, as part of a double extortion attack.

tools.name: Rubeus
tools.description: A tool commonly used for Kerberoasting attacks, likely used by Coinbase Cartel to harvest service account credentials from Aptim's network.

tools.name: Impacket
tools.description: A framework whose components, such as GetUserSPNs.py, are commonly used for Kerberoasting attacks. It was likely used by Coinbase Cartel against Aptim.

identities.name: Aptim
identities.description: A U.S.-based engineering, program management, and construction firm targeted by the Coinbase Cartel ransomware group.

infrastructure.name: Coinbase Cartel Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Coinbase Cartel group to post claims of attacks and threaten the release of exfiltrated data from victims like Aptim.

indicators.name: Potential Kerberoasting Activity (Event ID 4769)
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Kerberoasting Activity (Event ID 4769)
id: 1a9b8c7d-2e3f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects a high volume of Kerberos service ticket requests (Event ID 4769) from a single source, which is a strong indicator of a Kerberoasting attempt. This rule specifically looks for tickets requested for user accounts (not machine accounts) using the weaker RC4-HMAC encryption.'
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/blog/coinbase-cartel-infiltrates-engineering-leader-aptim
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-25
modified: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - attack.t1558.003
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate applications that query for multiple service tickets. Tuning may be required.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4769
    TicketEncryptionType: '0x17'
  filter:
    ServiceName|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects signs of a Kerberoasting attempt by monitoring for a high volume of Kerberos service ticket requests (Event ID 4769) for user accounts with RC4-HMAC encryption.

indicators.name: Rubeus Kerberoasting Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Rubeus Kerberoasting Tool Execution
id: 2b8c7d6e-3f4a-5b6c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Rubeus with the 'kerberoast' command, indicating an attempt to perform a Kerberoasting attack.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/blog/coinbase-cartel-infiltrates-engineering-leader-aptim
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-25
modified: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - attack.t1558.003
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate penetration testing or red team activity.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\Rubeus.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'kerberoast'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Rubeus tool with command-line arguments specific to performing a Kerberoasting attack.

indicators.name: Impacket GetUserSPNs Execution for Kerberoasting
indicators.pattern:
title: Impacket GetUserSPNs Execution for Kerberoasting
id: 3c7d6e5f-4a5b-6c7d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Impacket's GetUserSPNs.py script, indicating an attempt to perform a Kerberoasting attack.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/blog/coinbase-cartel-infiltrates-engineering-leader-aptim
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-25
modified: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - attack.t1558.003
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate penetration testing or red team activity.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'GetUserSPNs.py'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the GetUserSPNs.py script from the Impacket framework, used to identify accounts vulnerable to Kerberoasting.
